Title: Molecular Basis for Sesterterpene Diversity Produced by Plant Terpene Synthases

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes geranylfarnesyl diphosphate (GFPP) to a novel sesterterpene C1 with an 11-6-5 fused ring system via a type-B cyclization route; also previously shown to accept GGPP for diterpene formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C1 (novel sesterterpene; E/Z isomer of flocerene); dolabelladienol and other diterpenes with GGPP
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant of sesterterpene skeleton type; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables production of type-B plant sesterterpenes and was identified as the first type-B sester-TPS in plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli with AtGFPPS2 and MVA pathway; GC-MS; purification and NMR structure elucidation of product C1; site-directed mutagenesis of W335 and S503
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-a subfamily class I terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Low expression in seedling roots and flowers
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Not detected in tested Arabidopsis roots or flowers
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Arabidopsis grown at 22°C under 16-h light/8-h dark; enzyme function assayed in engineered E. coli supplied with mevalonolactone
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering type-B sesterterpene skeletons and studying TPS evolution; catalytic promiscuity toward GGPP and GFPP suggests utility for scaffold diversification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Native Arabidopsis metabolite accumulation was below detection or products may be further metabolized in planta
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: AtTPS06 is more similar phylogenetically to sesqui-TPSs than to other sester-TPSs and is proposed as a possible evolutionary intermediate/progenitor of type-A sester-TPSs; W335 and S503 are required for activity
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS06 was identified as one of three nonclustered functional Arabidopsis sester-TPSs. 2. It produces a novel type-B sesterterpene skeleton (C1) from GFPP and is the first plant type-B sester-TPS reported. 3. It is catalytically promiscuous, also using GGPP for diterpene synthesis. 4. W335 and S503 are essential residues for proper GFPP accommodation and activity.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP to sesterterpene product(s), but the major product structure was not elucidated because of low yield
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Terminal determinant of product skeleton; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers sester-TPS activity in Arabidopsis when coexpressed with AtGFPPS2
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli with AtGFPPS2 and MVA pathway; GC-MS detection of C25 products
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-a subfamily class I terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots and siliques
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Engineered E. coli system supplied with mevalonolactone
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential source of additional plant sesterterpene scaffolds, although product structure needs clarification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Low yield prevented NMR characterization of the main product(s)
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Identified as one of three nonclustered functional Arabidopsis sester-TPSs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS22 was identified as a functional Arabidopsis sester-TPS. 2. Sesterterpene products were detected only when coexpressed with AtGFPPS2 in engineered E. coli. 3. Its native expression is high in roots and siliques. 4. Product structural assignment remained unresolved due to low yield.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP to a novel sesterterpene C2 possessing a 5-8-6-5 fused ring system via the type-A route
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C2 (novel sesterterpene with 5-8-6-5 fused ring system)
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key terminal determinant of sesterterpene skeleton type; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes formation of a novel type-A sesterterpene scaffold in Arabidopsis-derived enzyme assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli with AtGFPPS2 and MVA pathway; GC-MS; purification and NMR structure elucidation of product C2
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-a subfamily class I terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in roots and siliques
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Not detected in tested Arabidopsis tissues by GC-MS full scan
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Engineered E. coli system supplied with mevalonolactone
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for engineering novel type-A sesterterpene backbones
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Native product was not detected in tested Arabidopsis tissues, possibly due to low abundance or further metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of three newly identified nonclustered functional Arabidopsis sester-TPSs
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS29 was identified as a functional Arabidopsis sester-TPS. 2. It produces a novel sesterterpene C2 with a 5-8-6-5 fused ring system. 3. The product backbone is consistent with type-A cyclization. 4. Native expression is high in roots and siliques, but the product was not detected in tested tissues.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP to multiple sesterterpenes, predominantly C7 ((+)-thalianatriene), through type-A cyclization; catalytic pocket architecture controls product specificity
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: C7 ((+)-thalianatriene) as major product; also minor C3-C6 and other products depending on mutant state
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control enzyme for sesterterpene skeleton formation and product fidelity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines type-A sesterterpene production and product specificity; structural features enlarge the catalytic pocket for GFPP and aromatic residues govern cyclization mode and fidelity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein crystallography with FSPP analog; docking; heterologous expression in engineered E. coli; GC-MS; site-directed mutagenesis; kinetic assay with purified GFPP; NMR of mutant-derived products
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-a subfamily class I terpene synthase with N-terminal plastidial transit peptide removed in recombinant constructs
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Engineered E. coli supplied with mevalonolactone; in vitro assays with synthetic GFPP; crystallization with FSPP
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Excellent chassis enzyme for rational engineering and directed evolution of new sesterterpene scaffolds; G328P increases productivity; F496 and other active-site residues tune product spectrum
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Many mutations reduce activity and product specificity; some substitutions abolish activity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Helix G outward shift enlarges catalytic pocket; N493 contributes via hydrogen bonds to helix G positioning; type-A sester-TPSs contain unusually high numbers of aromatic active-site residues; G328 and F496 strongly affect specificity; close homolog interconversion with AtTPS19 and Cru237 was partially achieved
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS18 structure revealed a larger catalytic pocket, mainly due to the outward shift of helix G, enabling GFPP accommodation. 2. Type-A sester-TPS catalytic pockets contain five aromatic residues, more than classic TPSs, and this feature helps define type-A cyclization. 3. N493 supports pocket expansion and activity, while G328 and F496 are major determinants of product fidelity and yield. 4. Mutants such as G328P increased sesterterpene production and generated new products, demonstrating utility for enzyme engineering. 5. Structural comparison enabled partial interconversion of product specificity with close homologs AtTPS19 and Cru237.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP predominantly to C8 ((-)-retigeranin B) and serves as a close homolog used for structure-guided interconversion with AtTPS18
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces a distinct major sesterterpene product from AtTPS18 despite high sequence similarity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli; GC-MS; site-directed mutagenesis and reciprocal residue swapping with AtTPS18
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-a subfamily class I terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Provides sequence-guided options to redirect AtTPS18-like enzymes toward alternative sesterterpene scaffolds
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reciprocal conversion with AtTPS18 was only partial and often reduced yield
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Residues corresponding to M352/I349, D431/Y428, and G461/D458 contribute to product divergence between AtTPS18 and AtTPS19; F493A abolished activity in supplemental analysis
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtTPS19 is a close homolog of AtTPS18 but produces C8 as the main product. 2. Three active-site residue differences contribute to product divergence between AtTPS19 and AtTPS18. 3. Structure-guided double and triple mutants partially interconverted product profiles, supporting the causal role of these residues.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cyclizes GFPP predominantly to C9 ((-)-caprutriene); single residue N353 helps define specificity relative to AtTPS18
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces a distinct sesterterpene scaffold from a close homologous TPS background
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in engineered E. coli; GC-MS; reciprocal mutagenesis with AtTPS18
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: TPS-a subfamily class I terpene synthase
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A single-residue switch (N353V) indicates strong potential for precision engineering of product outcome in homologous TPSs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reciprocal AtTPS18 V353N mutation caused inactivity, indicating strong context dependence
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: N353V in Cru237 converted activity to complete AtTPS18-like output, whereas AtTPS18 V353N was inactive
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Cru237 is a close homolog of AtTPS18 that mainly produces C9. 2. A single residue at position 353 is a major determinant of product specificity relative to AtTPS18. 3. Cru237 N353V completely switched to AtTPS18-like activity, but the reciprocal AtTPS18 V353N mutation abolished activity.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Prenyl diphosphate precursor supply for sesterterpene biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes geranylfarnesyl diphosphate (GFPP), the C25 prenyl donor used by sesterterpene synthases
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Essential precursor-supply enzyme in the heterologous system; rate limitation not tested here
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides GFPP required for detection of sesterterpene production by Arabidopsis and Capsella TPS enzymes in E. coli
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Coexpression with TPS genes in engineered E. coli followed by GC-MS detection of C25 products
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Engineered E. coli supplemented with mevalonolactone
Pathway_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: AtTPS06; AtTPS18; AtTPS19; AtTPS22; AtTPS29; Cru237
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key upstream module for metabolic engineering of plant sesterterpene production platforms through precursor boosting
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used as a coexpressed partner rather than the main mechanistic subject, but essential for the reported gene-metabolite mapping
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AtGFPPS2 supplied the GFPP precursor required for all heterologous sesterterpene production assays. 2. Coexpression with AtGFPPS2 enabled functional identification of multiple plant sester-TPS enzymes in E. coli. 3. It acts upstream of TPS enzymes by expanding the C25 precursor pool.
